neighborhoods.name: Centro Storico (Florence)
neighborhoods.description: Renaissance heart of Florence with the Duomo, Uffizi, Ponte Vecchio, and incomparable artistic heritage
neighborhoods.character: Renaissance, art capital, walkable and monumental

housing.predominantType: Apartments in Renaissance and medieval palazzi

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Lucca. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Fiesole
neighborhoods.description: Hilltop Etruscan town above Florence with Roman theater, monastery, and panoramic views of the city

housing.predominantType: Villas, farmhouse conversions, and elegant apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Lucca's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Prato / Pistoia
neighborhoods.description: Cities northwest of Florence with their own artistic heritage, lower costs, and fast train connections
neighborhoods.character: Art cities, affordable, train-connected to Florence

housing.predominantType: Apartments and houses at lower Florence-area prices

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Chianti (Greve / Radda)
neighborhoods.description: Rolling hill country between Florence and Siena with vineyards, olive groves, and international residents
neighborhoods.character: Wine country, rolling hills, international lifestyle

housing.predominantType: Renovated farmhouses, villas, and converted estates

neighborhoods.character_notes: Area with established international community and expat-oriented services. English is more commonly spoken here than in other neighborhoods.
